mission.title: Install and Configure Wasmedge Runtime on Kubernetes
mission.description: WasmEdge is a lightweight, high-performance WebAssembly runtime designed for cloud-native, edge, and decentralized applications. Installing WasmEdge allows you to run serverless apps, microservices, and smart contracts efficiently on various devices.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for WasmEdge
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for the WasmEdge runtime to keep your resources organized.

steps.title: Deploy WasmEdge Runtime
steps.description: Apply the WasmEdge deployment manifest to your Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description: Check if the WasmEdge pods are running correctly in the created namespace.

uninstall.title: Remove WasmEdge Runtime
uninstall.description: To uninstall WasmEdge, delete the deployment and the namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade WasmEdge Runtime
upgrade.description: To upgrade WasmEdge, apply the new version of the deployment manifest.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If the WasmEdge pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors using: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n wasmedge`.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter resource issues, ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated for the WasmEdge pods.

troubleshooting.title: Network Issues
troubleshooting.description: If the pods cannot communicate, verify the network policies and service configurations in the `wasmedge` namespace.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of WasmEdge will have all pods running in the 'wasmedge' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via the specified port. Resource limits should be configured as needed.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed on your local machine.
